name: Zee model (radiative neutrino mass)
summary: Generates Majorana neutrino masses at one loop via a charged scalar singlet h^+ and an extra Higgs doublet. Lepton-number-violating couplings combine with the Yukawa hierarchy to produce m_nu ~ (1/16 pi^2) (m_e m_tau / m_h^2) v^2 / Lambda, giving naturally tiny neutrino masses without a heavy seesaw scale. The original 1980 model is now largely excluded by oscillation data, but extensions remain viable.

addresses_puzzles.note: Loop suppression gives small m_nu without a high scale; the original Zee model with two equal Higgs doublets predicts a vanishing neutrino mass matrix diagonal element which clashes with observed mixing. Zee-Babu (1988) two-loop variant and minimal-Zee with broken Le-Lmu-Ltau remain alive.

touches_benchmarks.note: Majorana nature implies 0nubb at rates depending on the model details.
Charged scalar exchange generates mu -> e gamma at one loop; constrained by MEG.

introduces.note: Charged-scalar mass, mixing in two-doublet sector, and lepton-flavor structure of the antisymmetric f_ij Yukawa coupling.

history: Zee (1980), 'A theory of lepton number violation, neutrino Majorana mass, and oscillation', Phys. Lett. B 93, 389 (with erratum PLB 95, 461). Zee-Babu two-loop extension (1988). Original equal-doublet model excluded by oscillation data; modified variants with explicit Z_2 symmetry remain viable.

references.citation: Zee, 'A theory of lepton number violation and neutrino Majorana masses', Phys. Lett. B 93, 389 (1980)
Babu, 'Model of calculable Majorana neutrino masses', Phys. Lett. B 203, 132 (1988)
